title: Used VW Golf (Mk7) Buying Guide: Risks & What to Check | AutoVerdict
description: What to check before buying a used Volkswagen Golf Mk7 — DSG service, TSI oil use, diesel DPF, and the questions to ask the seller before you view it.
intro: The Mk7 Golf is one of Europe's most-bought used cars — well built, but the details that separate a sound example from an expensive one are easy to miss. Here is what to verify before you contact the seller.

faq.q: Is the VW Golf Mk7 a reliable used car?
faq.a: The Mk7 Golf is generally regarded as solid, but condition depends heavily on servicing — especially the DSG gearbox and, on diesels, the emissions system. A car with a full, stamped service history is a much safer bet than mileage alone suggests.

faq.q: What is the most common problem with a used Golf Mk7?
faq.a: Commonly reported areas to check include DSG gearbox behaviour (hesitation or jerkiness), oil consumption on some TSI petrol engines, and DPF/EGR issues on diesels used mainly for short trips. None are guaranteed faults — they are points worth verifying on the specific car.

faq.q: Petrol or diesel Golf — which should I buy used?
faq.a: For mostly city/short-distance driving, a TSI petrol usually avoids diesel emissions headaches. For high annual mileage and motorway use, a 2.0 TDI can make sense if it has been serviced on schedule. Match the engine to how you actually drive.
